title: Utility and Remaining Nodes (Viewer, Warning, Reroute, SetMaterial, Image)
description: Implement the remaining utility and infrastructure nodes. Viewer: marks a point in the graph for viewport display (the node whose output is rendered). Warning: emit a warning message to the user when a condition is met. Reroute: pass-through node for graph layout cleanliness. Frame: visual grouping of nodes (no computational effect). ObjectInfo: read metadata about the current object. SetMaterial: assign a material/color to geometry. ImageInfo: read dimensions and metadata of an image. ImageTexture: sample color from an image at UV coordinates. EdgePathsToSelection: convert edge paths to boolean selection. InputShortestEdgePaths: find shortest paths between vertices along edges.
definition-of-done: 1. Node classes in nodes/utility/. 2. Viewer marks the graph output point for rendering. 3. Warning stores a condition+message that the UI can display. 4. Reroute is a no-op pass-through for any port type. 5. SetMaterial assigns a color attribute to faces. 6. ImageTexture samples from a loaded image at UV coordinates. 7. InputShortestEdgePaths computes shortest path using Dijkstra on edge weights.
testing-plan: 1. Viewer on a cube graph: verify the cube is what renders in the viewport. 2. Reroute: wire a mesh through a reroute, verify identical output. 3. SetMaterial with red color: verify face color attribute is set. 4. Warning with always-true condition: verify warning message is stored. 5. InputShortestEdgePaths between two opposite cube vertices: verify path length is 3 edges.
unknowns: Whether ImageTexture should load from file or accept a texture handle from the rendering system
Whether SetMaterial should integrate with the MeshShader's existing material system or define a new one
